title: Ceriscoides perakensis
taxonomy: Ceriscoides perakensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Malay Peninsula. The classification of Ceriscoides perakensis places it in the order Gentianales. Within the family Rubiaceae, Ceriscoides perakensis is a member of the tribe Ixoroideae. This plant is also known to be a part of the genus Ceriscoides.

intro: Ceriscoides perakensis, also known as Perak oak, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. The Perak oak has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, thereby providing relief from pain, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ceriscoides perakensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Perak oak include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
